fields.icd_conflict_detail: All three documents consistently document ICD-10 code G12.21 (Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis) with no discrepancy found across the referral form, clinical notes, or prescription.
fields.priority_reason: Patient has a confirmed diagnosis of Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), a progressive and life-threatening neurological condition, making timely provision of the manual wheelchair clinically urgent to maintain mobility and patient safety.
fields.notes: 1. Clinical concern: The diagnosis is ALS (G12.21), a progressive neurodegenerative disease; however, the clinical notes reference a 'post-operative assessment' and 'wound healing within normal parameters,' which is inconsistent with an ALS diagnosis and may indicate a documentation error or template mismatch in the clinical notes — recommend clinical team review. 2. The treating physician is from Desert Orthopedics, which is not a typical specialty for ALS management; a neurologist referral or co-management may be appropriate. 3. Medical justification references 'safe ambulation,' but ALS patients typically have progressive paralysis — a manual wheelchair may become insufficient over time; power wheelchair needs should be assessed proactively. 4. A Tagalog interpreter must be arranged for delivery appointment. 5. Prescription signed 07/26/2026 is valid for 90 days (expires approximately 10/24/2026). 6. Occupational therapy has been recommended in conjunction with DME provision — confirm OT referral is active.

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026
